ucs.title: [LLM] WIRTE / Gaza Cybergang IronWind loader: regsvr32 DLL execution from LNK-in-archive lure
ucs.description: Hunts WIRTE's characteristic IronWind / SameCoin delivery chain in which a Hamas/Israel-conflict-themed phishing email delivers a ZIP containing a LNK that invokes regsvr32.exe (or rundll32.exe) against a sideloaded DLL dropped to %APPDATA% or %ProgramData%. This combines T1566.001 → T1204.002 → T1218.010 → T1574.001, which is the exact WIRTE 2023-2024 tradecraft documented by Check Point and Trustwave (Operation SilentSelfie / IronWind).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_process values(Processes.process_id) as pid from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe")) (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("explorer.exe","7zFM.exe","WinRAR.exe","Bandizip.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe")) (Processes.process IN ("*\\AppData\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\Temp\\*")) (Processes.process IN ("*.dll*","*.ocx*","*.cpl*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner process_guid [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\AppData\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Temp\\*") (Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.lnk" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.zip") by Filesystem.process_guid Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename process_guid as process_guid] | where match(process,"(?i)/s|/i:|\\\\") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql: let suspectFiles = DeviceFileEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed") | where FolderPath has_any ("\\AppData\\","\\ProgramData\\","\\Users\\Public\\","\\Temp\\") | where FileName endswith ".dll" or FileName endswith ".ocx" or FileName endswith ".lnk" | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("explorer.exe","7zg.exe","7zfm.exe","winrar.exe","bandizip.exe","outlook.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe") | project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, DroppedFile=FolderPath, FileName, DropParent=InitiatingProcessFileName; DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where FileName in~ ("regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe") | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("\\AppData\\","\\ProgramData\\","\\Users\\Public\\","\\Temp\\") | where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\.(dll|ocx|cpl)" | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("explorer.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","7zg.exe","winrar.exe") | join kind=inner suspectFiles on DeviceId | where Timestamp between (DropTime .. (DropTime + 1h)) | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessParentFileName, DroppedFile, FileName, ReportId | order by Timestamp asc
ucs.rationale: WIRTE's IronWind / SameCoin chain is reliably identified by the join of (a) a DLL/LNK dropped under AppData/ProgramData by an archive utility or browser/Outlook (post-LNK extraction) and (b) regsvr32 or rundll32 invoking that exact DLL within an hour, parented by explorer.exe. Generic LOLBin hunts fire on legitimate installers — requiring archive-utility parentage AND user-writable drop AND droppi

ucs.title: [LLM] WIRTE VBA-to-VBS-to-PowerShell stager beaconing on non-standard HTTPS port
ucs.description: Hunts WIRTE's long-running spearphishing chain (documented by Kaspersky 2019 onwards and S2 Grupo) where Arabic-language Office lures spawn wscript/cscript or PowerShell that beacons to actor-controlled HTTPS infrastructure on non-standard ports (T1571), which is a hallmark separating WIRTE from most other Gaza Cybergang sub-clusters. Catches T1566.001 → T1204.002 → T1059.005/T1059.001 → T1071.001/T1571.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("WINWORD.EXE","EXCEL.EXE","POWERPNT.EXE","OUTLOOK.EXE") Processes.process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_guid Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true count values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as remote_ip values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as remote_port values(All_Traffic.app) as app from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port!=80 All_Traffic.dest_port!=443 All_Traffic.dest_port!=53 (All_Traffic.dest_port=2096 OR All_Traffic.dest_port=2083 OR All_Traffic.dest_port=2087 OR All_Traffic.dest_port=8443 OR All_Traffic.dest_port=2053 OR All_Traffic.dest_port>=8000 AND All_Traffic.dest_port<=8999) by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest_ip All_Traffic.dest_port | rename All_Traffic.src as dest] | stats values(cmdline) as cmdline values(parents) as parents values(remote_ip) as remote_ip values(remote_port) as remote_port count by dest user | where count>0 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql: let officeStager = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe") | where FileName in~ ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","powershell.exe","mshta.exe","cmd.exe") | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http","https","FromBase64","DownloadString","IEX","Invoke-Expression","-enc","-EncodedCommand","WScript.Shell","Msxml2.XMLHTTP","WinHttp") or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\$\{?[a-z]\w*\}?\s*=\s*[\"'][a-zA-Z0-9+/=]{40,}" | project StagerTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, StagerProcess=FileName, StagerCmd=ProcessCommandLine, OfficeParent=InitiatingProcessFileName; let suspectBeacon = DeviceNetworkEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess" | where RemotePort !in (80,443,53,21,22,25,587,993,995) | where RemotePort between (1024 .. 65535) | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("powershell.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") | where not(ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP)) | project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine; officeStager | join kind=inner suspectBeacon on DeviceId | where NetTime between (StagerTime .. (StagerTime + 30m)) | summarize FirstSeen=min(StagerTime), LastBeacon=max(NetTime), Ports=make_set(RemotePort,20), IPs=make_set(RemoteIP,20), Cmds=make_set(StagerCmd,5) by DeviceName, AccountName, OfficeParent, StagerProcess | order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.rationale: The combination of (Office app → script-host child → outbound HTTPS to a non-443 high port within 30 minutes) is intentionally narrow. Most legit Office automation never beacons; most commodity stealers prefer 443. WIRTE/Ashen Lepus is specifically documented (Kaspersky 'WIRTE\'s campaign in the Middle East', S2 Grupo TNTGate report) using non-standard HTTPS ports such as 2096/8443/2087 from its V
